commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: So the demons besought him, saying, If you cast us out, allow us to go away into the herd of swine. "Send us into the herd of swine. "According to St. Luke, they begged of him two things; the first, that they might not be sent into hell, there to be tormented with more grievous torments, as they will be at the end of the world; the second, that they might be permitted to go into the herd of swine, that these being destroyed, the inhabitants of that country might be ill affected towards our Saviour, and refuse to receive him. The event seems to confirm this opinion. (Menochius).

commentaries.author: Peter Chrysologus
commentaries.content: Slaves ask for suitable indignities: “Send us into the swine.” Foulness begs to be sent from the tombs into the swine, so that it does not think of getting rid of its bad odors but of changing odors. “Send us into the swine.” Plunging from the sky, they seek filth. After living in the upper world, they look for the sloughs of pigs. “Send us into the herd.” A herd is sent into the herd, so that a gang of demons is brought forth, and it seems that two men have caused what a great number of pigs could not endure.
